skill.name: component-model-analysis
skill.description: Evaluate framework extensibility patterns, abstraction layers, dependency injection, plugin systems, and configuration strategies.
skill.summary: Analyze how a framework supports customization through abstractions, dependency injection, plugins, and configuration.
skill.author: Dowwie

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks, an example API key field, and keyword matches as potential risks. Manual review found only documentation examples, with no executable scripts, network calls, secret access, malware intent, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported backticks are markdown code fences around illustrative Python, YAML, and markdown examples. They are not Ruby shell backtick execution and do not instruct the agent to run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are fenced documentation snippets or plain integration notes. No executable shell command, command interpolation, or instruction to run these examples is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Example API Key Field Flagged as Secret Access
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The api_key reference is an illustrative dependency injection configuration field. It does not read environment variables, expose credentials, or send secrets anywhere.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line appears inside a Python example showing container configuration. There is no process environment access, file read of secrets, or network transmission.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Keyword Matches Flagged as Blockers
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are keyword matches in the skill description and configuration discussion. No cryptographic implementation, host enumeration, or system probing behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows architecture-analysis prose and headings. I did not find evidence of cryptographic code, system reconnaissance, or malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Framework extensibility is hard to compare when abstractions, dependency injection, and configuration are undocumented. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a structured component model review.
actual_capabilities: Classifies framework abstractions as thick, thin, or mixed.
Identifies constructor, factory, registry, and container dependency injection patterns.
Compares code-first, config-first, and hybrid configuration strategies.
Maps extension mechanisms for custom models, tools, memory, and plugins.
Produces a structured markdown analysis with recommendations.
limitations: Requires source code or prior codebase mapping for accurate analysis.
Does not execute framework code or verify runtime behavior.
Does not replace hands-on architecture review for complex systems.
Focuses on extensibility design, not performance or security testing.

use_cases.title: Select an Agent Framework
use_cases.description: Compare how candidate frameworks support custom components before adoption.

use_cases.title: Review Extension Design
use_cases.description: Assess whether a framework uses clear interfaces, registries, factories, or containers.

use_cases.title: Document Architecture Tradeoffs
use_cases.description: Create a concise report explaining customization difficulty and configuration choices.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Component Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this framework component model. Identify its main base classes, dependency injection style, configuration strategy, and extension points.

prompt_templates.title: Extensibility Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Structured review for a project decision
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the provided codebase for extensibility. Classify abstractions as thick, thin, or mixed, then explain how difficult custom components are to add.

prompt_templates.title: Configuration Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the framework configuration approach. Explain whether it is code-first, config-first, or hybrid, and describe the impact on testing and operations.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Framework Matrix
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these frameworks with a component model matrix. Compare abstraction depth, dependency injection, plugin registration, validation, and customization risks.

output_examples.input: Review an agent framework with base classes for models, tools, and memory.
output_examples.output: A markdown report classifies each component, identifies the primary injection pattern, lists extension mechanisms, and recommends where interfaces should be thinner.

output_examples.input: Compare two frameworks for custom tool support.
output_examples.output: A comparison highlights registry design, factory behavior, testability, configuration formats, and the relative difficulty of adding custom tools.

output_examples.input: Document whether a framework is code-first or config-first.
output_examples.output: A configuration section explains the dominant strategy, supported formats, validation approach, and tradeoffs for developers and non-developers.

best_practices: Run codebase mapping first so component names and inheritance paths are available.
Provide representative framework files, not only high-level documentation.
Ask for recommendations only after the component and configuration model is mapped.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill as a replacement for executing tests or examples.
Do not compare frameworks without providing equivalent source areas.
Do not treat thick abstractions as bad without checking their intended lifecycle behavior.

faq.question: What does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes framework abstractions, dependency injection, extension points, and configuration strategy.

faq.question: Does it run code?
faq.answer: No. It provides a guided static architecture review based on files and context.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it compare multiple frameworks?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure a comparison when each framework has comparable source context.

faq.question: What should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide source files, class maps, configuration examples, or output from a codebase mapping skill.

faq.question: Is this a security audit skill?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on architecture extensibility and configuration design, not security testing.
